title: Skill Artifact
description: Extension schema for artifacts of kind 'skill'. Skills are portable prompt-and-script bundles that agents can incorporate. Skills have no runtime of their own -- they are consumed by agents or AI-powered IDEs.

content.description: Content definition for the skill, including entrypoint, scripts, references, assets, and trigger phrases (Tier 1, optional).

bom.description: Skill-specific Bill of Materials describing dependencies, tool requirements, and model requirements (Tier 1 optional, Tier 2 required).

SkillContent.description: Content bundle for a skill artifact.

entrypoint.description: Path to the primary skill definition file (typically 'SKILL.md'). This file contains the prompt instructions that define the skill's behavior.

scripts.description: Supporting scripts used by the skill.

assets.description: Static assets bundled with the skill (templates, config files, data files).

triggerPhrases.description: Natural-language phrases that should trigger activation of this skill. Used for skill discovery and routing. Maximum 20 phrases, each max 256 characters.

Script.description: A supporting script included in the skill bundle.

path.description: Relative path to the script file.

Reference.description: An external or bundled reference material.

Asset.description: A static asset bundled with the skill.
